creator: fernape@FreeBSD.org
text:
(In reply to Alexey Dokuchaev from comment #7)

> Of course it does, it just needs config and theme files as it says in the log.  > I've changed the port so it copies default system-wide ones under ~/.config
>/gorss to reduce first-time user's confusion.

It really doesn't.
And I had copied the config and the themes. By the way, the config in the example gives an error which I don't remember exactly but was something along "repeated key" or something like that. I suppose you also get it.

>No it doesn't really /need/ it, only as a last resort.  There's probably 
> something wrong with your environment; to start with, what's the value of $TERM?

Not really.

$ echo $TERM
screen-256color

I just happen to use tmux.
